core_payload.content:
# Agent Control Plane

## 1. Role
Agent Control Plane is the administrative substrate through which one operator discovers, supervises, and governs a large N1Hub swarm across web, terminal, mobile, and daemon surfaces.

## 2. Governance Surface
- searchable activity views across all agent lanes
- unified session and assignment discovery across clients and daemon contexts
- programmatic policy rollout and canonical definition paths
- state-aware audit trails for canonical and projected side effects
- lane budgeting for API, subscription, foreground, and night-shift work
- escalation and notification routing for failures, clarifications, and review requests

## 3. Assignment and Continuity Layer
The control plane treats assignments, artifacts, ownership, and return paths as first-class objects. Swarm growth remains intelligible because session history, budget posture, artifact lineage, and runtime health project into one authoritative administrative surface instead of fragmenting across hidden local files or ad hoc dashboards.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Agent Control Plane supports To Dig Deep by making swarm scale come with accountability, inspectability, and economic discipline rather than invisible sprawl.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Control Plane defines the administrative surface that lets one operator discover, supervise, and govern a large N1Hub swarm across web, terminal, mobile, and daemon contexts. It covers searchable activity views, assignment discovery, policy rollout, audit lineage, budget posture, failure escalation, and notification routing as one coherent governance shell. The capsule matters because swarm growth stays inspectable and economically legible when session history, ownership, artifact lineage, and runtime health project into a single control surface instead of scattering across hidden files or ad hoc dashboards.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so the capsule states current doctrine directly.
